title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides multiple interfaces including a desktop app (2.0), IDE, CLI, and SDK to support flexible agentic development workflows.
writeup: This video from Google Antigravity compares the four surfaces of its agent-first software development platform: the 2.0 desktop app, the CLI, the IDE, and the SDK. It frames the choice around agentic development workflows where developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. The takeaway is that each interface targets a different mode of work, from desktop and IDE environments to command-line and programmatic SDK access, so the right surface depends on the workflow at hand.
